claims.summary: Meta-analysis of 8 RCTs (n=606) shows significant reduction in inflammatory markers and pain scores in osteoarthritis. Effect size comparable to NSAIDs with better safety profile.

clinical_protocol.standard: RCTs used standardized curcumin extracts (500-1000mg daily of curcuminoids, often with piperine for bioavailability). Theracurmin, Meriva, and BCM-95 are common formulations.
clinical_protocol.traditional_equivalence: Traditional use involves 1-3g fresh turmeric root or 1-2 tsp powder in food, often with fat (ghee, coconut oil) and black pepper. This provides ~100-200mg curcuminoids, far less than studied extracts.

provenance.commercial_drift_note: Modern clinical trials use standardized curcumin extracts (500-1000mg curcuminoids daily) often with bioavailability enhancers (piperine, liposomal, nanoparticle). Traditional dietary turmeric provides ~100-200mg curcuminoids from 1-3g root. Marketing often cites extract study results for dietary turmeric or low-dose supplements without noting the 5-10x dose difference.

claims.summary: Traditional use as digestive stimulant; limited modern clinical data. Small RCTs suggest benefit in dyspepsia and IBS.
No modern clinical trials for antioxidant claims. Traditional use as general health tonic across Ayurveda, TCM, and Southeast Asian traditions.
